title: Course Schedule
coreIdea: Determine if all courses can be finished given prerequisite pairs, which is equivalent to checking if the directed graph of dependencies contains a cycle. If no cycle exists, a valid topological order exists.
recognitionCues: asks if a valid order exists or if completion is possible
approach: Build adjacency list and in-degree count for each course. Use Kahn's algorithm: start with all zero in-degree nodes in a queue, process each by removing its edges and decrementing neighbors' in-degree, adding new zero in-degree nodes to queue. If all nodes are processed, no cycle exists (answer true); otherwise a cycle exists (answer false).
